slides.title: A New Paradigm 🎯
slides.content:
# Object-Oriented Programming

**OOP** organizes code around "objects" that combine:

* 📦 **Data** (attributes)
* ⚙️ **Behavior** (methods)

Think of objects like real-world things!

slides.title: Real-World Objects 🌍
slides.content:
# Objects Are Everywhere!

**A Car object might have:**

*Attributes (data):*
* color = "red"
* speed = 60
* fuel = 50

*Methods (actions):*
* start()
* accelerate()
* brake()

quizData.question: What do objects combine?
quizData.explanation: Objects bundle data (attributes) and behavior (methods) together!

slides.title: Classes: The Blueprint 📐
slides.content:
# Classes vs Objects

**Class** — The blueprint/template
```python
class Dog:  # Blueprint
    pass
```

**Object** — An instance of the class
```python
my_dog = Dog()  # Actual dog!
fido = Dog()    # Another dog!
```

slides.title: Class Preview 💻

quizData.question: What is a class?
quizData.explanation: A class is a blueprint — objects are instances created from that blueprint!

slides.title: Why OOP? 🤔
slides.content:
# Benefits of OOP

* 🔄 **Reusability** — Create many objects from one class
* 📦 **Encapsulation** — Hide complexity
* 🧬 **Inheritance** — Build on existing code
* 🔧 **Maintainability** — Easier to modify

slides.title: OOP in Python 🐍
slides.content:
# Everything is an Object!

```python
x = 5          # int object
s = "hello"    # str object
lst = [1,2,3]  # list object
```

In Python, every value is an object with attributes and methods!

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Think in Objects

When designing programs, ask:

* What are the *things* (nouns)? → Classes
* What do they *have*? → Attributes
* What can they *do*? → Methods

quizData.question: In Python, is 5 an object?
quizData.explanation: In Python, EVERYTHING is an object — even integers and strings!
